item.title: Growing Oil Glut Spurs Asian Refiners to Offer Cargoes to the US
item.description: Oil output from Persian Gulf nations is ramping up so quickly as the Strait of Hormuz reopens that Asian refiners, already adequately supplied, are pushing some crude to destinations as far afield as California.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Picks Up as Supertankers Sail Into Persian Gulf
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picked up for the first time since Iran’s recent attacks on ships in the waterway, with more operators sending crude tankers into the Persian Gulf.

item.title: MSC Unit to Invest $1.4 Billion in Adani’s Vizhinjam Port
item.description: A unit of Mediterranean Shipping Co. will invest $1.4 billion to acquire a 49% stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Pvt., marking one of the largest foreign commitments in India’s port infrastructure.

item.title: Aluminum Set for Worst Monthly Loss Since 2008 on Supply Outlook
item.description: Aluminum headed for the steepest monthly loss since 2008 as expectations for a return of lost Middle Eastern supply saw it rapidly unwind an Iran war-induced rally.

item.title: RBI Funding Curbs Deal ‘Body Blow’ to Indian Prop Trading Firms
item.description: India’s proprietary trading firms and stock brokers are bracing for a major overhaul of how they finance trading activity.

item.title: Trump Lost the Fed Battle, But Look What He Won
item.description: The Supreme Court cleared his right to fire regulators at will — like in markets, crypto.

item.title: Singtel CEO’s Pay Cut 17% to $5.3 Million After Network Outages
item.description: Singapore Telecommunications Ltd. cut Group Chief Executive Officer Yuen Kuan Moon’s pay by 17% after a year marked by emergency service disruption and network outages in Australia and Singapore.

item.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Sale Sees Strong Demand on BOJ Caution
item.description: Japan’s two-year government bond auction Tuesday saw stronger demand than the 12-month average, reflecting concerns over the Bank of Japan’s policy path.

item.title: Indonesia Capital Flight Caps Most-Profitable Bank’s Wealth Push
item.description: Indonesia’s biggest non-state bank said capital flight has hampered efforts to expand its wealth management business, underscoring how an erosion of investor confidence is hindering the private sector in Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

item.title: Taiwan Opposition’s $7.5 Billion Plan Stirs Drone Defense Debate
item.description: Taiwan’s main opposition party outlined plans to develop the drone industry just days after stalling a similar proposal from President Lai Ching-te’s government, amid a debate on unmanned systems with crucial implications for the island’s defense.

item.title: China Broker Stocks Eye Best Month Since October on Trading Boom
item.description: Chinese brokerage stocks are set to close June with their strongest monthly gain in eight months, driven by rising trading activity and IPO issuance that may sustain the rally.

item.title: Indonesia Biofuel Push Adds Strain to Producers and Palm Markets
item.description: The rollout of a pioneering palm-diesel blend is set to stretch Indonesian biofuel makers to their limits and tighten global supplies of the tropical oil by diverting it away from export markets.

item.title: Record Bond Inflows Ease India Equity Exodus
item.description: Global funds invested $5.2 billion in index-eligible bonds this month.

item.title: Amazon to Pay $2.25 Million to End US Case Over ID Theft Victims
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. agreed to pay $2.25 million to settle US claims that it routinely failed to provide records to help customers who were victims of identity theft.

item.title: Indonesian Bonds Draw $1.2 Billion as Higher Yields Lure Funds
item.description: Indonesian bonds are on track for their biggest monthly inflow in more than a year, in a sign that government efforts to lift yields to lure foreign investors and support the rupiah are paying off.

item.title: Pakistan Buys Emergency LNG Shipment on Disrupted Qatar Exports
item.description: Pakistan purchased a liquefied natural gas shipment for prompt delivery this week as tension in the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt Qatari exports to the South Asian nation.

item.title: Smuggler Pleads Guilty in $30 Million Scheme to Move Immigrants
item.description: A criminal network generated up to $30 million from smuggling immigrants across the southern border into the US, according to a statement from the Justice Department.

item.title: China Clamps Down on Issuance of Higher-Yielding Offshore Debt
item.description: Chinese authorities are discouraging firms from raising money offshore at higher yields, in the latest step to clamp down on risks among municipal borrowers shouldering more than $100 billion of overseas bond debt, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: China’s Factory Activity Returns to Growth as Exports Boom
item.description: China’s factory and non-manufacturing activity fared better than expected in June, as booming exports offset cooling growth in the domestic economy.

item.title: StanChart Former AI Chief Joins Accenture as Southeast Asia Head
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc’s former global head of AI enablement, David Hardoon, has joined Accenture Plc as managing director and head of advanced AI for Southeast Asia.

item.title: Mizuho, SMFG Fuel Record Japanese Corporate Bond Sales Overseas
item.description: Mizuho Financial Group Inc. and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc. propelled foreign-currency bond sales by Japanese companies to a record for any quarter Monday as the nation’s companies tap deeper overseas markets to finance growth.

item.title: New Zealand Firms More Confident as Fuel Prices Ease, ANZ Says
item.description: New Zealand companies have responded positively to early signs of a US-Iran peace deal and indications of cheaper fuel prices, a survey showed.

item.title: South Korea to Speed Nuclear Push to Meet Surging Power Demand
item.description: The South Korean government will consider ways to cut atomic power construction times as it looks to ramp up energy supply to meet the demands of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Katayama Maintains ‘Stable’ Tone Even as Yen Hits 40-Year Low
item.description: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said Japan will respond to developments in foreign exchange appropriately at any time, staying in tune with her recent remarks even after the yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986.

item.title: How Ambani Built Project Jupiter to Launch India’s Biggest IPO
item.description: Last August, Mukesh Ambani promised shareholders that Jio Platforms Ltd. would seek a stock market listing in the first half of 2026.

item.title: South Korea Tightens Property Curbs as Chip Boom Spurs Home Prices
item.description: South Korea imposed tougher property curbs in neighborhoods outside Seoul with proximity to semiconductor hubs after a boom in the industry fueled sharp home-price gains, raising concerns about speculative buying.

item.title: AirPods Maker Luxshare Seeks Up to $3.1 Billion From Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. has started taking investor orders for its Hong Kong listing that may fetch as much as HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion), the city’s biggest share debut so far this year.

item.title: Tencent Ramps Up Buybacks as Share Rout Wipes Out $309 Billion
item.description: China’s Tencent Holdings Ltd. is ramping up buybacks as its Hong Kong-listed shares struggle to recover from a selloff that’s wiped out about $309 billion in market value since early October.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Warns of Global Oil Glut, Cuts Outlook on Hormuz
item.description: Morgan Stanley cut oil forecasts for the second time in about two weeks as flows through the Strait of Hormuz return faster than expected, while strong US supply and weak Chinese demand raise the risk of a glut.

item.title: Gold Holds Drop as US-Iran Tensions Weigh on Inflation Outlook
item.description: Gold held a decline as traders weighed conflicting signals from the US and Iran ahead of fresh talks to end the war that’s fueled global inflationary pressures.
